---
title: 'Whiskey Cake Kitchen &amp; Bar'
date: '2013-03-18T20:36:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'LIFE in Review (s)'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '8072977684442349076'
blogger_filename: '/2013/03/whiskey-cake-kitchen-bar.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/LIFE in Review (s)/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Discover'
---

<br /><h1 data-mce-style="text-align: center;" style="font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 2.4em; font-weight: normal; line-height: 1.5em; margin: 0px 0px 20px; text-align: center;"><b>Whiskey Cake Kitchen &amp; Bar</b></h1><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"></div><div style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><br /></div><div style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px;"><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/whiskey-cake-526ebefcd306.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="clear: right; float: right; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-left: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/whiskey-cake-bafd2a2a0a49.jpg" height="240" width="320" /></a></div></div><div style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px;">As we walk past the covered patios and see the fire from the kitchen within behind glass behind the bar;&nbsp;<b style="color: black;">Whiskey Cake</b>&nbsp;is already promising to be a unique dining experience. We then walk in the double set of double doors into the restaurant and are greeted by warm and friendly hosts. The first thing I noticed was the firewood which doubled as fuel for the kitchen and decoration as well.</div><div style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px;">As we are walking through to our table we pass rocking chairs and other unique seating arrangements. We are seated at a booth with industrial lightening made from pipes and wires. Everything about this restaurant is first class yet home-grown. It's a true<b style="color: black;">&nbsp;farm to garden experience</b>. They even recycle as much as possible. The napkin rolls are held together by large plumbing fittings. The coasters are cut up squares from cardboard boxes and stamped with the logo. There is this feeling that&nbsp;<b style="color: black;">current</b>&nbsp;(fresh, organic, all natural, clean, west coast style) meets&nbsp;<b style="color: black;">farm fresh</b>&nbsp;(down home, comfortable, easy-going, raw, rustic, old west).</div><div style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px;">As we are seated we are handed menus. Not just any menus either. They are one page. No flipping for pages and pages to find 100's of items. All the drinks, apps, food, salad, burgers... one page. It is located on an equally sized cardboard cut out for to add firmness and clipped together with office clips.</div><div style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px;">They create their&nbsp;<b style="color: black;">food from scratch</b>&nbsp;made from all&nbsp;<b style="color: black;">fresh/never frozen</b>&nbsp;ingredients. All the vegetables are from right here in texas and even the herbs used for seasoning are grown right on property in their greenhouse. They fly in fresh meats from around the world as well. I had an amazing piece of Scottish Salmon!</div><div style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px;">We started our meal with a special treat of&nbsp;<b style="color: black;">Friend Egg Plant</b>&nbsp;and dipping sauce. I then turned to my&nbsp;<b style="color: black;">Live Wood Grilled Salmon</b>. It was served over wilted spinach, shiitake mushrooms, and cherry tomatoes. This was all mixed with a shallot vinaigrette. My friend had the&nbsp;<b style="color: black;">Mesquite Grilled Redfish</b>. Both were fresh and perfectly prepared. Topping that meal off was our Whiskey Cake; served warm. It was crispy on the outside and soft in the middle served over a creamy sugary sauce.</div><div style="margin-bottom: 24px;"><span style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, Lucida Grande, Verdana, sans-serif;">It was a an&nbsp;</span><b><span style="font-family: Helvetica, Arial, Lucida Grande, Verdana, sans-serif;">organic farm fresh food and&nbsp;home-style&nbsp;experience.</span></b><span style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, Lucida Grande, Verdana, sans-serif;">&nbsp;But it was also&nbsp;</span><b style="color: black; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px;">high quality food and thoughtfully created.</b><span style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, Lucida Grande, Verdana, sans-serif;">&nbsp;One can tell from the presentations and preparations that there is a first class chef behind it all.</span></div><div style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div><div style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px;">Well done Whiskey Cake! DW<br /><br /><h4 style="text-align: center;">Photos:</h4><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><iframe align="center" frameborder="0" height="500" scrolling="no" src="http://www.flickr.com/slideShow/index.gne?group_id=&amp;user_id=106439236@N07&amp;set_id=72157639316237063&amp;text=" width="500"></iframe></div></div><div style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px;"><small>Created with <a href="http://www.admarket.se/" title="Admarket.se">Admarket's</a> <a href="http://flickrslidr.com/" title="flickrSLiDR">flickrSLiDR</a>.</small><br /><br /><div style="text-align: center;"><img alt="Whiskey Cake on Urbanspoon" data-mce-src="/images/imported/www-urbanspoon-com/biglink-aba8ac220699.gif" data-mce-style="border: none; padding: 0; width: 200px; height: 146px;" src="/images/imported/www-urbanspoon-com/biglink-aba8ac220699.gif" style="border: none; color: black; font-family: 'Times New Roman'; font-size: medium; height: 146px; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; width: 200px;" /></div><div style="text-align: center;"><br /></div><i style="border: none; color: inherit;">*Special thanks to my friend&nbsp;<b style="color: black;">Brent Wickham</b>&nbsp;for finding and taking me to this very cool place!&nbsp;</i></div><div style="color: #333333; font-family: Helvetica, Arial, 'Lucida Grande', Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px;"><i style="border: none; color: inherit;">*&nbsp;I didn't have my camera so I'll be borrowing pictures from their own Facebook page to help with the descriptions.</i><br /><i style="border: none; color: inherit;"><br /></i><i style="border: none; color: inherit;"></i><br /><div style="color: black; font-family: 'Times New Roman'; font-size: medium; font-style: normal;">Please comment below if you have anything to add, say, or correct.<br /><br /><b>Texas Living Info by&nbsp;</b><b><span style="font-size: x-small;">Darrell G. Wolfe</span></b><br /><br /><h4>Blog:</h4><ul><li><a href="http://texaslivinginfo.blogspot.com/" target="_blank">http://texaslivinginfo.blogspot.com/</a></li></ul><br /><h4>Connect:</h4><div style="clear: both; float: left; padding-bottom: 10px;"><a href="https://www.facebook.com/DarrellGWolfe" imageanchor="1"><img alt="Follow me on Facebook" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/facebook-icon-436ed0b74b58.png" style="border: 0px; height: 32px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 32px;" />&nbsp;</a><a href="https://twitter.com/DarrellWolfe"><img alt="Follow  &lt;b&gt;YOUR-TWITTER-ACCOUNT-NAME&lt;/b&gt; on Twitter" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/twitter-logo-0d130abda9d2.png" style="border: 0px; height: 33px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 33px;" />&nbsp;</a><a href="http://www.youtube.com/user/darrellgwolfe"><img alt="Follow YOUR-CHANNEL-ID on YouTube" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/youtube-icon-df42b17a5900.jpg" style="border: 0px; height: 33px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 33px;" />&nbsp;</a><a href="http://feeds.feedburner.com/TexasLivingInfo" rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml"><img alt="RSS subscription icon" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/rss-logo-d57cc3305733.jpg" style="border: 0px; height: 33px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 33px;" />&nbsp;</a>&nbsp;&nbsp; </div><br /><br /><h4></h4><h4>Disqus:</h4><div><b>What is YOUR favorite place to go?</b></div><br /><div id="disqus_thread"></div><a class="dsq-brlink" href="http://disqus.com/">comments powered by&nbsp;<span class="logo-disqus">Disqus</span></a><br /><br />____</div></div>
